episode_title: Zach Seward: How a Five-Person AI Team Is Powering Innovation at The New York Times
transcript_file: 2025-05-30 - Zach Seward How a Five-Person AI Team Is Powering Innovation at The New York Times.txt
summary_claims: A local reporter today might have to cover 20 cities, whereas 15 years ago it might have been one or two

Local government agencies often contract with just a handful of platform companies for agendas and minutes, making scalable scraping possible by targeting the platforms rather than individual agencies
Santa Clara County, in the heart of Silicon Valley, had the most ICE contracts in California despite not being a border county
Silence in public meetings predicted newsworthiness, which was unexpected (in addition to shouting)

guests.name: Cheryl Phillips
guests.role: Founder and Co-Director of Big Local News

matches.theme_name: The Automation Paradox: What AI Replaces, What Remains Irreplaceable

best_quote.text: It's really centered about around lowering the cost of doing accountability journalism, especially for local newsrooms, which as we know are resource strapped.
best_quote.speaker: Cheryl Phillips

matches.summary: AI enables local reporters covering 20+ cities (vs. 1-2 fifteen years ago) by automating government meeting monitoring, entity extraction from police records, and layoff notice scraping. Agenda Watch, Autofolio, and WARN Act scrapers represent task-specific automation that frees human journalists for source-calling and investigation. Human verification remains essential -- AI code is described as 'running with scissors' without coding literacy.

matches.theme_name: Trust on the Line: Credibility, Transparency, and the Audience Contract

best_quote.text: As the agent does its work, it tells you what it's doing. So you ask that question. And it says thinking and it tells you all the steps it's doing. I'm going to this database, I'm going to this table, this table, this table, this table... And then it explains what it did, both in plain English, and it gives you the code.
best_quote.speaker: Cheryl Phillips

matches.summary: DataTalk's step-by-step transparency (showing SQL steps, plain English explanations, caveats, shareable dashboards) embodies transparency-as-trust-mechanism. Starling Lab's blockchain authentication of body camera footage and Reuters photos represents a concrete provenance system. Deep fakes and AI-generated misinformation are identified as threats requiring authentication infrastructure.

matches.theme_name: The Ethics Infrastructure: From Principles on Paper to Practice in the Newsroom

best_quote.text: We want to avoid people going and saying, Oh, this must be right because I asked the AI and the AI said. And so that's where we try to really introduce those critical thinking skills and a little code literacy goes a long way.
best_quote.speaker: Cheryl Phillips

matches.summary: Phillips advocates teaching coding fundamentals before introducing AI tools, delaying AI access until three-quarters through the curriculum. This represents an ethics-through-education approach: building critical evaluation skills as the foundation for responsible AI use. The DART Matrix framework embeds responsible practices by matching tools to capability levels.

matches.theme_name: Building the Machine: Technical Architecture and Engineering Decisions

best_quote.text: As the agent does its work, it tells you what it's doing. So you ask that question. And it says thinking and it tells you all the steps it's doing.
best_quote.speaker: Cheryl Phillips

matches.summary: Rich technical diversity: SUQL (Structured and Unstructured Query Language) powers DataTalk's natural language data querying across 10 FEC tables; Autofolio performs generative AI entity extraction from multiformat police records; Starling Lab uses blockchain for content provenance with hashing, notarization, and P2P storage; audio analysis ML detects newsworthiness via volume spikes and silence. Open-source is consistently emphasized (Hugging Face, Stanford Oval agent).

matches.theme_name: The Literacy Imperative: Upskilling the Newsroom for an AI Era

best_quote.text: I think it's a little bit like running with scissors, so you have to be careful. But I do think there's a lot of potential.
best_quote.speaker: Cheryl Phillips

matches.summary: Phillips teaches at Stanford and structures AI literacy carefully: coding fundamentals first, AI tools introduced later, critical evaluation emphasized throughout. The DART Matrix framework assesses newsroom data capacity and matches support accordingly. Story recipes for rural newsrooms with zero data experts represent a threshold-lowering strategy. Students submitting perfect AI-generated code signals the need for pedagogical adaptation.
matches.matched_text: I think it's a truism that if people are gonna be getting us on their Claude chat, then we'll need to be there. And I don't think it's necessarily it doesn't have to look or feel like Like the times, I would assume you'd want it to.

matches.theme_name: Organizing for AI: Leadership, Strategy, and Institutional Transformation

best_quote.text: It increases the impact for the big data teams if they share it back out, and it makes it possible for the small data teams to be able to cover stories they would otherwise completely miss.
best_quote.speaker: Cheryl Phillips

matches.summary: The DART Matrix provides a strategic framework for matching AI tools to newsroom capability levels. Big Local News itself is an organizational model (university-newsroom partnership) but newsroom-level leadership and change management are not central topics.

matches.theme_name: Power, Money, and the Platform: Big Tech and the News Industry

best_quote.text: It increases the impact for the big data teams if they share it back out, and it makes it possible for the small data teams to be able to cover stories they would otherwise completely miss.
best_quote.speaker: Cheryl Phillips

matches.summary: Big Local News is fundamentally a collaborative model: university-based infrastructure shared with 40+ newsrooms in the California Reporting Project, Baltimore Banner, Honolulu Civil Beat, and others. The DART Matrix creates a virtuous cycle between big and small data teams. Open-source emphasis (Hugging Face, Stanford Oval) counters platform dependency on proprietary AI companies.

matches.theme_name: Reimagining the News Product: Personalization, Formats, and the Experience Layer

best_quote.text: As the agent does its work, it tells you what it's doing.
best_quote.speaker: Cheryl Phillips

matches.summary: DataTalk's shareable dashboards represent a new format for data journalism. WARN Act Slack alerts and Audit Watch tips are new delivery mechanisms. But these are journalist-facing tools, not audience-facing product innovations. The Starling Lab's clickable provenance interface is a reader-facing format innovation.

matches.theme_name: Survival Economics: Business Models, Revenue, and Market Position in the AI Era

best_quote.text: It's really centered about around lowering the cost of doing accountability journalism, especially for local newsrooms, which as we know are resource strapped.
best_quote.speaker: Cheryl Phillips

matches.summary: The entire episode addresses the local news crisis: reporters covering 20+ cities, resource constraints preventing accountability journalism, funding limitations on scaling federated police oversight systems. Big Local News lowers the cost of accountability journalism for resource-strapped local newsrooms by sharing data infrastructure, story recipes, and pre-built charts. This is the most detailed treatment of the local news resource crisis in the sample.

matches.theme_name: Regulating the Revolution: Law, Policy, and Journalism as Accountability

best_quote.text: We were able to identify moments in public meetings, like several years worth of public meetings where city county officials essentially proclaimed that they were not sharing data with ICE, even though they had just inked new contracts with ICE, like the day before.
best_quote.speaker: Cheryl Phillips

matches.summary: Journalism as accountability mechanism is the episode's central purpose: AI-enabled cross-referencing of ICE contracts with public meeting transcripts catches officials in contradictions; police use-of-force database creation holds 700+ agencies accountable; federal audit monitoring detects financial distress in publicly-funded entities. Starling Lab's blockchain provenance provides content authentication standards. Government data disappearing from the web is framed as a governance crisis.
matches.matched_text: we were able to to take as we had no desktop traffic to worry about, cannibalizing. So the same thing's gonna happen with AI related news startups, but I think you're right to to suggest like that doesn't change anything about the fundem underlying business models of the industry being any in any better shape than they have been.

matches.theme_name: The Prototype Pipeline: From Experiment to Shipped Product

best_quote.text: It's really centered about around lowering the cost of doing accountability journalism, especially for local newsrooms, which as we know are resource strapped.
best_quote.speaker: Cheryl Phillips

matches.summary: Agenda Watch in beta aiming to scale from hundreds to 3,000 agencies in six months. Autofolio in pilot for entity extraction. WARN Act scraper and DataTalk in production. The episode shows projects at multiple maturity stages. Scaling challenge: 700+ police agencies in California alone, with funding constraints limiting expansion.

matches.theme_name: Data Privacy, Source Protection, and the Boundaries of Responsible Use

best_quote.text: We want to avoid people going and saying, Oh, this must be right because I asked the AI and the AI said.
best_quote.speaker: Cheryl Phillips

matches.summary: Data preservation is a central concern: police agencies can delete public records after a set period, requiring proactive collection; government data is disappearing from the web. Starling Lab's blockchain authentication addresses data provenance and integrity. The messy, multiformat nature of police records (PDFs, audio, video) makes data quality a persistent challenge.
